messages.text: The Game Club room sits in the old school building, across the connecting corridor from the main building.

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: Welcome to the Game Club!
The backlight from the window makes it hard to see inside at first. As my eyes adjust, I take in the room.
Monitors and game consoles fill about half a classroom. Two people are already here.
A boy with glasses and cold, narrow eyes sits at a PC. A gyaro-style girl with brown hair lazily blows bubble gum while mashing a fighting game controller.

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: Let me introduce everyone. The cool-looking guy with glasses is our vice president, Kase Gakuto-senpai.

messages.name: Gakuto
messages.text: ...Oi, Tendou.

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: And the stylish older girl buried in her fighting game — well, she is always playing — is Oiso Nina-senpai.
The atmosphere in the room intimidates me. Kase-senpai's cold gaze alone could make anyone shrink.

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: Anyway, I want you two to see our club activities first. Kase-senpai, please stop brooding and just play some FPS like you always do.

messages.name: Gakuto
messages.text: Oi, Tendou. I have never once in my life approached an FPS with a casual mindset.
It is the latest entry in a famous FPS series I have dabbled in. Kase-senpai silently sets the CPU difficulty to maximum.
The match ends in the blink of an eye. Tasuku and I die so many times it is almost embarrassing.

messages.name: Gakuto
messages.text: One more.
I blink. He cuts me off before I can react.

messages.name: Gakuto
messages.text: Play another match. Hurry up and get ready.
So the second match happens. Then the third. Kase-senpai stays in first place... but Tasuku has climbed so close to Tendou in second that it is almost a tie.
I watch Tasuku's hands on the controller. He seems to observe every movement and absorb techniques in real time. His talent is undeniable.

messages.name: Gakuto
messages.text: You are not bad. What is your name?

messages.name: Tasuku
messages.text: Ah, I am Tasuku.

messages.name: Gakuto
messages.text: Tasuku, you show promise. At first you could barely control your character... but you kept improving. Impressive.
Tendou nods. This is exactly the quality needed to grow at gaming — the drive to steal techniques, the right attitude, and sharp observation. Tasuku has all three at a high level.
Then Kase-senpai turns toward me. His eyes are full of harsh criticism.

messages.name: Gakuto
messages.text: Compared to him, what are you doing? You remember the basic controls since you have played before... but across three matches, I saw zero growth from you.

messages.name: Gakuto
messages.text: Also — you kept jumping on top of that vehicle in the middle of the map. What was that about?

messages.name: Keita
messages.text: Ah, because... from up there, the scenery looked really beautiful. So I tried jumping to see it better. And it was amazing! ...Although I got shot immediately after.
Kase-senpai lets out a scornful laugh. He turns away, all competitive interest gone. The pressure crushes my chest.
I feel frustrated with myself. He criticized me harshly, and maybe he was right. But still — that scenery really was something.
